sections.paragraph:
& שֶׁבֶט with suff. שִׁבְטִי, pl. שְׁבָטִים, constr. שִׁבְטֵי m. (once f. Eze 21:15), a staff stick, rod, so called from supporting: (to this answer, σκήπτων, σκῆπτρον, σκηπίων, scipio, scapus, Germ. Schaft); specially-

(1) used for beating or striking, Isa 10:15 14:5 and chastening (virga), Pro 10:13 13:24 22:8 hence שֵׁבֶט אֱלֹוהַּ the rod with which God corrects (used of calamities sent by God), Job 9:34 21:9 37:13 Isa 10:5.- Isa 11:4, שֵׁבֶט פִּיו “the rod of his mouth,” i.e. severe sentences. [But see 2Th 2:8.]

(2) a shepherd’s rod a crook, Levit. 27:32 Psa 23:4.

(3) the sceptre of a king, Gen 49:10 [this belongs to No. 4]; Num 24:17 Psa 45:7 Isa 14:5 Amo 1:5, and of a leader, Jud. 5:14 whence-

(4) a tribe of the Israelites (so called from the sceptre of the leader or prince of the tribe, see מַטֶּה No. 3 ), Exo 28:21 Jud. 20:2 sometimes also i.q. מִשְׁפָּחָה family, Num 4:18 Jud. 20:12 1Sa 9:21.

(5) a measuring rod, and meton. a portion measured off, Psa 74:2 Jer 10:16 51:19 .

(6) a spear (which consists of a staff or rod, with a spear-head put at the top), 2Sa 18:14. Compare מַטֶּה No. 2.
